prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "直腿跳" (straight-leg pogo hops) exercise, side view, facing right. Person posture: Athletic person CLEARLY AIRBORNE mid-flight during a stiff-legged pogo hop. The figure is OFF THE GROUND — both feet are visibly above the floor with empty space between feet and ground. BOTH LEGS ARE NEARLY FULLY EXTENDED AND STRAIGHT (only a minimal knee bend, almost completely locked-out, RIGID like the pogo stick). Both legs are parallel and held side-by-side. ANKLES are PLANTARFLEXED (toes pointing DOWN at the floor like a ballerina). The whole body is rigid and vertical, like a pogo stick. Arms held loosely at the sides with slight elbow bend for balance. Torso completely upright. This is a reactive ankle-bouncing drill — the only joint that bends is the ankle. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: knees are LOCKED-OUT STRAIGHT (NOT bent like in running), the body is AIRBORNE OFF THE GROUND, both ankles are pointed downward.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: calf muscles (gastrocnemius and soleus) for elastic recoil and ankle stiffness. Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: quadriceps and core muscles for body rigidity. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腓肠肌 / 比目鱼肌" pointing to the calf muscles for elastic recoil; "股四头肌" pointing to the quadriceps; "核心肌群" pointing to the core muscles. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
